geographiclib 0.4.2.9002
  • Reference
  • Articles
  • Changelog

geographiclib CRAN Release Notes

Version: 0.3.7 Date: 2026-02-19

Pre-submission Audit Summary

R CMD check –as-cran: PASS

All substantive checks pass. The only NOTEs are: - New submission (expected) - Missing pdflatex / tidy on local machine (environment-only, not package issues) - Installed size 18.2MB on debug build (stripped release build is ~1.6MB)

Compilation

  • Zero compiler warnings across 43,000 lines of C++ (24 wrapper files + vendored GeographicLib)
  • No use of sprintf, cout, cerr, printf, exit, abort in wrapper code
  • Vendored GeographicLib cout statements are behind if constexpr (false) — dead code eliminated at compile time
  • Compiles cleanly with g++ -std=gnu++17 (R 4.5 default)
  • No SystemRequirements or CXX_STD needed — uses only C++11/14 features, compiles under R’s default C++17 standard

Documentation

  • 74 exported functions, all with roxygen2 documentation
  • 26 man pages (using @rdname grouping)
  • 5 vignettes, all build cleanly
  • All examples run in <10ms each
  • No \dontrun{} usage

Tests

  • 912 tests via testthat (edition 3)
  • 0 failures, 0 warnings, 0 skips
  • Total test time: ~3.5 seconds

DESCRIPTION

  • Title: Access to 'GeographicLib' (no trailing space, not starting with package name)
  • Description: Starts with Bindings to the 'GeographicLib' C++ library (not the package name)
  • Authors@R:
    • Michael Sumner (cre, aut)
    • Charles Karney (cph, aut) — GeographicLib author
    • Mark Borgerding (cph) — kissfft library (BSD-3-Clause), used internally by DST
  • License: MIT + file LICENSE (compatible with vendored MIT/X11 and BSD-3-Clause code)
  • URL and BugReports both set
  • RoxygenNote: 7.3.3

Package Size

  • Source tarball: ~550KB
  • Installed (debug): 18.2MB (libs 17.4MB due to debug symbols)
  • Installed (release, stripped): ~2MB estimated
  • No bundled data files

Code Quality

  • No print(), cat(), message(), warning() in R function bodies
  • No installed.packages(), require(), library() in R code
  • No modification of global state (options(), Sys.setenv())
  • No file system writes
  • cpp11 registration file is current (85 registered C++ functions)
  • NAMESPACE is in sync with roxygen2 output

NA Handling

  • Newer wrappers (DMS, Intersect, NearestNeighbor, GeoCoords) have explicit NA handling
  • Older wrappers rely on GeographicLib’s graceful NaN propagation (NaN inputs produce NaN outputs without crashing)
  • No segfaults from NA input in any function (tested)

Third-Party Code

All vendored in src/:

Library License Copyright Notes
GeographicLib 2.x MIT/X11 Charles Karney Core library
kissfft BSD-3-Clause Mark Borgerding Used internally by DST (FFT)

Both licenses are GPL-compatible and permissive.

Steps for Submission

Pre-submission (done)

Platform Checks (TODO - manual steps)

Submission

Potential CRAN Reviewer Concerns

  1. Large number of vendored C++ files — Standard practice for packages wrapping C++ libraries (cf. RcppEigen, nloptr). All source is MIT/BSD licensed.

  2. Installed size — Debug builds are large but stripped release builds are ~2MB. CRAN builds strip debug symbols automatically.

  3. Overlap with geosphere — geographiclib provides higher precision geodesic calculations and many more features. Not a drop-in replacement.

Historical Note: geosphere CRAN Issues

TODO: Review geosphere’s CRAN submission history for issues that might apply: - https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/geosphere/ - Check NEWS/ChangeLog for CRAN-forced changes - Common issues for geodesic packages: floating-point edge cases at poles, antimeridian, equator; platform-dependent precision differences

Contents

Developed by Michael Sumner, Charles Karney.
